canonical_definition: Tabular classification is defined in the provided literature as the task of predicting a categorical label for a data point from a set of structured, tabular features. The task is operationalized and measured using statistical methods, with one paper employing the Wilcoxon signed-rank test for evaluation. It is studied in relation to in-context and few-shot learning. Research on this task also investigates methods for improving sample efficiency, with one study reporting that test-time training "significantly reduces the required sample size for tabular classification" ("Test-Time Training Provably Improves Transformers as In-context Learners").
papers: Quantifying Prediction Consistency Under Fine-tuning Multiplicity in Tabular LLMs
TabFlex: Scaling Tabular Learning to Millions with Linear Attention
Test-Time Training Provably Improves Transformers as In-context Learners

definitions.text: The task of predicting a categorical label for a data point represented by a set of structured, tabular features.
definitions.paper: Test-Time Training Provably Improves Transformers as In-context Learners

iclr25: Mixture of In-Context Prompters for Tabular PFNs
